name: Tadalafil augmented doxazosin hypotension more than tamsulosin in healthy men
statement: In healthy normotensive men, tadalafil 20 mg significantly augmented the standing systolic blood-pressure effect of doxazosin 8 mg, while tadalafil 10 or 20 mg had little hemodynamic interaction with tamsulosin 0.4 mg in the abstract.
population_or_context: Healthy normotensive men receiving alpha-blockers under study conditions.
time_horizon: Blood pressure and heart rate recorded before dosing and for 24 hours after dosing.
quantitative_note: Doxazosin plus tadalafil produced a 9.8 mm Hg greater mean maximal standing systolic BP decrease versus doxazosin plus placebo; standing SBP below 85 mm Hg occurred in 28% with doxazosin plus tadalafil versus 6% with doxazosin plus placebo. With tamsulosin, mean maximal standing SBP decreases were similar to placebo and no subject had standing SBP below 85 mm Hg.

source_locator.section: PubMed abstract Materials and Methods, Results, and Conclusions
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports BP outlier and mean maximal standing SBP differences for doxazosin and tamsulosin.

caveats: The label still advises caution with alpha-blockers as a class.
This finding does not prove all tamsulosin combinations are safe in patients with BPH, older age, dehydration, or antihypertensive polypharmacy.
The studies were small and abstract-only extraction was used.
population_profile: Healthy normotensive men; reasonably direct for healthy men age 30 to 50 but less direct for BPH patients or people using alpha-blockers for hypertension.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 20 mg with doxazosin 8 mg; tadalafil 10 or 20 mg with tamsulosin 0.4 mg.
indication_context: Clinical pharmacology interaction study relevant to ED/BPH and off-label users taking alpha-blockers.
endpoint_measure: Standing systolic BP mean maximal decrease and standing SBP below 85 mm Hg outliers.
effect_estimate: Doxazosin interaction: +9.8 mm Hg mean maximal standing SBP decrease and 28% versus 6% SBP below 85 mm Hg outliers; tamsulosin interaction: mean differences 1.7 and 2.3 mm Hg, no SBP below 85 mm Hg.
safety_findings: Alpha-blocker co-use risk is not uniform; doxazosin showed a clinically relevant hypotension signal with tadalafil, supporting label recommendations for stability and lowest-dose initiation.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate to high for healthy men using alpha-blockers; limited for men with cardiovascular disease, BPH, volume depletion, alcohol use, or multi-drug antihypertensive exposure.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Small sample; healthy volunteer setting; abstract-only extraction; not powered for rare syncope or injury outcomes.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil safety or interaction evidence; transfer from other PDE5 inhibitors is not needed, but monitored trial or clinical-population context limits off-label healthy-user risk estimates.
